text:
Good evening, friends. Happy to be here tonight again in the service of the Lord. After the fine shower today on the outside, I trust that God will give us a shower on the inside, a spiritual shower. We sing sometimes, "Showers Of Blessings." That's what we need, isn't it? Great showers of blessings.
It's usually kind of in line to give blessings, or to pass what we call the bouquets at the end of the services, but this case, we won't have to do that. I never had the privilege of meeting personally any of the ministers and so forth, of the Association, who let us have this campgrounds for this little occasion that we've had these two nights. But if any of the trustees, or anyone connected with it, with letting us have this place, we want to thank you, kind brother, with all of our hearts. [Blank spot on tape.] And we'll start the prayer meeting.
text_no:
God kveld, venner. Det er en glede å være her igjen i Herrens tjeneste i kveld. Etter dagens fine regnskur utendørs, håper jeg Gud vil gi oss en åndelig regnskur innendørs. Vi synger noen ganger "Velsignelsens Regnskurer." Det er det vi trenger, ikke sant? Store regnskurer av velsignelser.
Vanligvis deler vi ut takk og ros mot slutten av møtene, men denne gangen trenger vi ikke gjøre det. Jeg har aldri hatt privilegiet av å møte noen av prestene og andre fra foreningen, som lot oss bruke denne leirplassen for de to kveldene vi har hatt. Men hvis noen av styremedlemmene eller andre involvert i å la oss bruke dette stedet er her, vil vi takke deg, kjære Bror, av hele vårt hjerte. [Tomt område på lydbånd.] Vi vil nå starte bønnemøtet.

text:
And remember… You love me? If you do, raise up your hands. And I'll raise both mine up: I love you in Christ Jesus, undying love. Pray for me.
Now, the One who can open the Scripture is not a man; it's God. So, may He open this blessed Word as we read from Saint Luke the 2nd chapter for a subject, and a text from another place. But in Saint Luke 2, about 25, we read this:
And, behold, there was a man in Jerusalem, whose name was Simeon; … the same was just and devout, and he waited for the consolation of Israel: and the Holy Ghost was upon him.
And it was revealed unto him by the Holy Ghost, that he would not see death, before he had seen the Lord's Christ.
And he came by the Spirit into the temple: and when they brought … the child Jesus, to do for him according to the custom of the law,
Then took he him up in his arms, and blessed God, and said,
Lord, now lettest … thy servant depart in peace, according to your words:
For my eyes have seen thy salvation.
In Romans 8 and the 14th verse:
For as many … are led by the Spirit of God, they are sons of God.
text_no:
Og husk... Elsker du meg? Hvis du gjør det, løft opp hendene dine. Jeg løfter begge mine: Jeg elsker deg i Kristus Jesus, en udødelig kjærlighet. Be for meg.
Nå, den eneste som kan åpne Skriften er ikke et menneske; det er Gud. Måtte Han åpne dette velsignede Ord mens vi leser fra Evangeliet etter Lukas, kapittel 2, som utgangspunkt, og en tekst fra et annet sted. Men i Evangeliet etter Lukas 2, vers 25, leser vi dette:
Og se, det var en mann i Jerusalem, som hette Simeon; … han var rettferdig og gudfryktig, og ventet på Israels trøst: og Den Hellige Ånd var over ham.
Og det var blitt åpenbart for ham av Den Hellige Ånd, at han ikke skulle se døden før han hadde sett Herrens Kristus.
Han kom ved Ånden til templet: og da foreldrene brakte inn barnet Jesus for å gjøre med Ham etter lovens skikk,
Tok han Ham opp i armene sine, og priste Gud, og sa:
Herre, nå lar Du Din tjener fare herfra i fred, etter Ditt Ord:
For mine øyne har sett Din frelse.
I Romerne 8, vers 14:
For så mange som ... er ledet av Guds Ånd, det er Guds sønner.

text:
Now, we will use for our subject tonight, "Expectations." And we'll use for our text tonight, "Leading of the Spirit of God."
In the days of our text, it was quite a time in Jerusalem. The Israelites, the Jewish people had fallen away from their God, and had went out, and got formal and indifferent, claiming all the days of miracles were passed. And they read what Joshua done. They read was Moses done. But they had got down because there had been an increase in science, and in the people. Education had become a predominant thing.
It's too bad that those things has to substitute for the Holy Spirit. God have mercy.
I believe… I say this with reverence; I say this with respect, and not using it for a crutch to support my ignorance: but I say that education has been one of the greatest curses that the religion of Jesus Christ has ever had. People substitute it for salvation. They think because the minister is very highly educated, speaks his words very well… What does God care about how you speak your words?
text_no:
I kveld vil vi bruke "Forventninger" som vårt tema. Teksten vår for kvelden er "Ledelse av Guds Ånd".
I dagene som er beskrevet i vår tekst, var det en spesiell tid i Jerusalem. Israelittene, det jødiske folket, hadde vendt seg bort fra Gud. De hadde blitt formelle og likegyldige, og hevdet at miraklenes tid var forbi. De leste om hva Josva og Moses hadde gjort. Men folket hadde bortskjemt seg selv, da vitenskapen og utdanning hadde fått økt betydning.
Det er beklagelig at slike ting må erstatte Den Hellige Ånd. Gud, miskunn Deg.
Jeg mener, og dette sier jeg med ærefrykt og respekt, ikke som en unnskyldning for min egen uvitenhet: Utdanning har vært en av de største forbannelsene for Jesu Kristi religion. Folk erstatter det med frelse. De tror at fordi en forkynner er høyt utdannet og taler godt, så har det betydning. Hva bryr Gud seg om hvordan du taler?

text:
Here some time ago, in Fort Wayne, Indiana, I was amazed. It just comes to my mind now. I walked behind a pulpit, where you that read this famous book, "We The People." Just the outstanding things of the world takes place; they put it in this. On the religious article, they wrote something about my services at Fort Wayne, where God made a total blind girl to receive her sight. And a baby that had its feet clubbed in was made completely whole at the platform.
I'd gotten real weak. They had taken me behind the stage where my friend, Paul Radar, that went to glory many years ago, had wrote the famous song that I pack with me now, "Only Believe." Standing in there, an educated man come in, very scholarly. From the sound of his talk, he must have taught Mr. Webster what to say. He said, "Brother Branham," said, "there's one thing wrong with your ministry."
I said, "Well, kind sir, I would like to know if there's anything wrong." I said, "I don't mean for it to be."
He said, "That is your grammar." Said, "Oh, it's poorly."
I said, "Yes, sir; that is right." I said, "There was nine of us children, boys, and one girl, ten children. My father died young." I said, "I had to work and take care of ten children." And I said, "I didn't get much education, just the seventh grade."
He said, "But, Brother Branham, you're a man now." Said, "That don't count now." Said, "You could take a correspondence or something, and check up on your grammar." Said, "It's terrible."
I said, "Yes, sir. I know that." I said, "I'm sorry. But," I said, "after the Lord has ministered to me, and I must go minister to Him, the people crowd so much in the… Right today, four hundred major cities in the United States is calling for services. Signed papers and things where tens of thousands attend the meeting." And I said, "How could I go, sir, like that?"
text_no:
For en tid tilbake, i Fort Wayne, Indiana, ble jeg forbløffet. Jeg kom nettopp til å tenke på det. Jeg gikk bak en talerstol der du som leser denne berømte boken, "We The People." Bare de mest bemerkelsesverdige hendelsene i verden blir inkludert der. I en religiøs artikkel skrev de om mine møter i Fort Wayne, der Gud ga en helt blind jente synet tilbake. En baby med klumpfot ble også fullstendig helbredet på plattformen.
Jeg hadde blitt veldig svak, og de tok meg bak scenen der min venn Paul Radar, som gikk til herlighet for mange år siden, hadde skrevet den berømte sangen jeg bærer med meg, "Only Believe." Mens jeg stod der, kom en velutdannet mann inn, svært lærd. Ut fra måten han snakket på, kunne det virke som han hadde lært Mr. Webster hva han skulle si. Han sa: "Bror Branham, det er én ting galt med din tjeneste."
Jeg svarte: "Vel, kjære sir, jeg vil gjerne vite om det er noe galt." Jeg sa: "Det er ikke meningen at det skal være det."
Han sa: "Det er grammatikkken din. Den er svært dårlig."
Jeg sa: "Ja, sir; det stemmer. Vi var ni barn, gutter, og en jente, ti barn totalt. Min far døde ung." Jeg sa: "Jeg måtte jobbe og ta vare på ti barn." Og jeg sa: "Jeg fikk ikke mye utdanning, kun til syvende klasse."
Han sa: "Men, Bror Branham, du er en voksen mann nå. Det teller ikke lenger. Du kunne ta et korrespondansekurs eller noe, og lære deg grammatikk." Han sa: "Den er forferdelig."
Jeg sa: "Ja, sir. Jeg vet det." Jeg sa: "Jeg beklager. Men," jeg sa, "etter at Herren har betjent meg, og jeg må betjene Ham, så blir folk så stappet inn i... Akkurat i dag kaller fire hundre større byer i USA på møter. Underskrevne papirer og ting der titusener deltar på møtene." Og jeg sa: "Hvordan kunne jeg få tid til det, sir?"

text:
"Oh," he said, "one thing that you made such a mistake on," said, "the night you said, 'All the people coming passed this polepit … polepit…'" He said, "Brother Branham, the congregation would appreciate you more if you said, 'pulpit,' and not 'polepit.'"
Well, he kind of pulled my ear a little hard, I guess. I said, "My beloved brother, I differ with you. Them people don't care whether I say, 'polepit,' or 'pulpit.' The thing they want me to do is preach the Word, and live what I'm talking about." I said, "That's the main thing."
So, Christ doesn't come by education. Christ comes by a surrendered heart believing Him. Now, education's fine, but it will never substitute salvation. It can't.
text_no:
"Åh," sa han, "det er én ting du gjør feil." Han sa, "Den kvelden du sa, 'Alle de som passerer denne talerstolen… talerstolen…'" Han sa, "Bror Branham, menigheten ville satt mer pris på det om du sa 'preikestol' i stedet for 'talerstol.'" 
Vel, han dro meg vel i øret litt hardt, antar jeg. Jeg sa, "Min kjære bror, her er vi ikke enige. De menneskene bryr seg ikke om jeg sier 'preikestol' eller 'talerstol.' Det de ønsker er at jeg forkynner Ordet og lever som jeg preker." Jeg sa, "Det er det viktigste."
Så, Kristus kommer ikke gjennom utdanning. Kristus kommer gjennom et overgitt hjerte som tror på Ham. Utdanning er fint, men det kan aldri erstatte frelsen. Det kan ikke.

text:
But, in them days, the people had got away. As soon as education comes in, the phenomenon leaves the Church. Christ leaves. He can't stand it. They get so smart they know more than He does. So, the Scripture is not given by education; it's given by revelation; not education. Revelation. So, as soon as education takes your pulpit…
Many of you people in selecting your ministers to come to your pulpit, you select the young fellow who's just out of school, who's smart and witty, a social mixer. God don't want a mixer; He wants a separator. God wants something that's… See? And you choose him, because a lot of times he's very smooth. He's kind, like… God don't choose men like that.
text_no:
Men i de dager hadde folket kommet på avveie. Så snart utdannelse kommer inn, forlater fenomenet Menigheten. Kristus forlater. Han kan ikke tåle det. De blir så kloke at de mener de vet mer enn Ham. Skriften gis ikke ved utdanning; den gis ved åpenbaring. Åpenbaring, ikke utdanning. Så snart utdanning tar plassen i din talerstol ...
Mange av dere, når dere velger deres forkynnere til talerstolen, velger den unge mannen som nettopp er ferdig med skole, som er smart og vittig, en sosial blander. Gud vil ikke ha en blander; Han vil ha en som skiller. Gud vil ha noe som er... Ser dere? Og dere velger ham, fordi han ofte er meget glatt. Han er vennlig, liksom... Gud velger ikke menn som det.

text:
Why, when Samuel went up to choose one out of David's boys … or, not David, but out of Jesse's boys (David's father), they brought out the oldest son when they knowed he was going to take Saul's place, great, big, fine looking, curly-headed, seven foot tall… Why, Jesse said, "He'll look wonderful in his kingly robes, and with his big crown sitting on his head."
You see how man's ideas can get into it? And when he went forward, the prophet thought, That may be the man.
He carried the cruise of oil; when he went forth, the Holy Ghost said, "I have refused him."
The world had chose him, but God refused him. The world looked on the outside; God was looking on the in.
They brought the next big one. Polished him up, perhaps combed his hair back. Said, "What a beautiful young man. Look how straight and sturdy he is. And we take him up and see what the Holy Spirit will say about him. I'm sure it'll be him."
When they brought him up there, Samuel took the cruise of oil and went forth to anoint him, because he was such a handsome, smart looking fellow. And the Holy Spirit said, "I have refused him." Till he brought all of Jesse's sons, the five up, and the Holy Spirit said, "I have refused them."
Saul … Samuel said, "Isn't there one more you have?"
He said, "Yes, I've got a little old ruddy, scrawny looking fellow back there taking care of the sheep; not very much about him."
"Go get him."
And when they brought him up, a little old fellow with a sheepskin coat on, hair hanging down his neck, and probably he said, "his, hain't," and "fetch, and carry, and tote," and no education, sheepstick in his hand. The Holy Ghost said, "I've chosen him. Go anoint him." See what the difference what men want and what God wants? See, it's the difference. Don't choose according to the outside; choose the inside. Inside's eternal; outside will perish.
text_no:
Da Samuel gikk for å velge en av Jesses gutter til å ta Sauls plass som konge, begynte de med den eldste sønnen. Han var stor, kjekk, krøllete og nesten to meter høy. Jesse sa: "Han vil se fantastisk ut i kongelige klær og med en stor krone på hodet." Ser dere hvordan menneskelige ideer kan påvirke dette? Da profeten så sønnen, tenkte han: Dette må være mannen.
Han bar med seg salveoljen, men Den Hellige Ånd sa: "Jeg har forkastet ham." Verden hadde valgt ham, men Gud avslo ham. Verden så på det ytre, men Gud så på hjertet.
Deretter brakte de den neste store sønnen. Kanskje polerte de ham opp og greide håret tilbake. "For en vakker ung mann. Se hvor rett og sterk han er. Vi tar ham og ser hva Den Hellige Ånd sier om ham. Jeg er sikker på at det må være ham," sa de. Da Samuel skulle salve ham, fordi han var så kjekk og kraftfull, sa Den Hellige Ånd: "Jeg har forkastet ham."
Dette skjedde med alle Jesses fem sønner. Den Hellige Ånd sa: "Jeg har forkastet dem."
Samuel spurte: "Har du ikke en sønn til?"
Jesse svarte: "Jo, jeg har en liten rødlig, spinkel gutt som passer sauene. Ikke mye å skryte av."
"Send etter ham," sa Samuel.
Da de hentet ham, så Samuel en liten gutt med saueskinnsjakke, håret hengende ned i nakken, uten utdannelse, og med en fårestav i hånden. Den Hellige Ånd sa: "Jeg har valgt Ham. Salve Ham." Ser dere forskjellen mellom hva mennesker ønsker og hva Gud ønsker? Velg ikke etter det ytre; velg etter det indre. Det indre er evig; det ytre vil forgå.

text:
But, when men begin to look on that, they choose their leaders. They choose their pastors, and refuse the old-time preaching of the cross, and the old-time salvation and altar calls. When you do that, your church is gone. That's right. Whether it's Baptist, Methodist, Presbyterian, or wherever it may be, it's gone.
Israel had done that: chosen their kings until they'd got away from God. They forgot about the power God gave David, about Moses. It was something they was reading about. And they had got all broke up then, and scattered about. What a perfect type it is of today, the same thing: choosing the mental theology, choosing men who can speak with great eloquent words. Why, they're speaking in unknown tongues to half of their audience. That's right.
text_no:
Men når mennesker begynner å fokusere på slikt, velger de sine ledere og pastorer, og avviser den gamle tidens forkynnelse om korset, frelsen og alterkallene. Når du gjør det, er menigheten tapt. Og det gjelder uansett om det er Baptist, Methodist, Presbyterianer, eller noe annet; det er tapt.
Israel hadde gjort det samme: valgt sine konger til de hadde fjernet seg fra Gud. De glemte kraften Gud ga David og Moses. Det var noe de leste om, men ikke levde etter. De var splittet og spredd. Dette er et perfekt bilde på dagens situasjon, hvor det samme skjer: man velger mental teologi og talere med stor veltalenhet. De taler jo i ukjente tunger for halve sitt publikum. Det stemmer.

text:
A fellow not long ago was trying to discuss with me about divine healing, and I knew he couldn't stand it in the Scripture. And he begin to use such big words; I said, "I have not the gift of interpretation, so speak to me in English, that I might know what you're speaking about." So, that is it.
Now, and when they did, they'd got away: got away from God, got away from the supernatural. "Oh, those things were past." But you know, God has never left Himself without a witness somewhere. It's got down sometime to one man, for instance, Noah, who found grace in the sight of God.
It's got down many times to one man, but God has always had a witness that He could put His hands on and say, "This is My servant. I tell him to go here, and he goes. I tell him to do this, and he'll do it, regardless of what the church says or what the people says; he'll do it anyhow." God's had a witness.
text_no:
For en tid tilbake forsøkte en mann å diskutere guddommelig helbredelse med meg, og jeg visste at han ikke kunne støtte sin argumentasjon med Skriften. Han begynte å bruke store ord, og jeg sa: "Jeg har ikke tolkeevnen, så snakk til meg på engelsk, så jeg kan forstå hva du snakker om." Så, slik er det.
Når de gjorde det, fjernet de seg fra Gud og det overnaturlige. "Å, de tingene var forbi." Men du vet, Gud har aldri latt seg være uten et vitne. Noen ganger er det redusert til én mann, for eksempel Noah, som fant nåde i Guds øyne.
Det har ofte vært redusert til én mann, men Gud har alltid hatt et vitne Han kunne legge Sin hånd på og si: "Dette er Min tjener. Jeg sier til ham å gå dit, og han går. Jeg sier til ham å gjøre dette, og han gjør det, uavhengig av hva menigheten eller folket sier; han gjør det likevel." Gud har alltid hatt et vitne.

text:
And in that day, He had a witness. And one of them was our man tonight, Simeon, an old sage, great long white beard, white flowing hair hanging down from under his turban. And he was spiritual. He believed in the phenomenal. He believed God. And one day while he was in prayer, God, by the Holy Spirit, revealed to him that he wasn't going to see death before he seen the Christ Child. What a revelation!
Now look. Four thousand years they had looked for Christ, and then all teed up in education, smart in science, and smart in psychology. Oh, they had it all under control, but was far away from the days of miracles; but God found one man He could talk to. God, give us another.
Old…
text_no:
Den dagen hadde Han et vitne. En av dem var vår mann i kveld, Simeon, en gammel vismann med langt, hvitt skjegg og hvitt, flytende hår som hang ned fra under turbanen. Han var åndelig og trodde på det fenomenale. Han trodde på Gud. En dag mens han var i bønn, åpenbarte Gud, ved Den Hellige Ånd, for ham at han ikke skulle se døden før han hadde sett Kristusbarnet. For en åpenbaring!
Tenk deg dette. Fire tusen år hadde de ventet på Kristus, og i mellomtiden var folk blitt opptatt av utdanning, flinke i vitenskap og dyktige i psykologi. Å, de hadde alt under kontroll, men de var langt borte fra miraklenes tid. Likevel fant Gud én mann Han kunne snakke med. Gud, gi oss en til.
Gammel...

text:
Now, what do you think people said to that old man, one foot in the grave, as we'd call it today, eighty-something years old, white beard, and going around testifying everywhere, saying, "I'm not going to die until I see the Lord's Christ."? Why? He had a right to believe it. It was revealed to him by the Holy Spirit. That's it: spiritual revealed truth. God said, "Upon this rock I will build My church, and the gates of hell can't prevail against it." Showed the gates of hell would be against it, but they can't prevail, when it was revealed to him that Jesus was the Christ of the apostle. That's it.
Notice, he believed God. Now, maybe the old fellow being a teacher, great reputation, smart man, educated… What do you think his fellow preachers thought about him when he went around telling the people that he was going to see the Christ before he died?
Why, they'd say, "It's no time for Christ. We'll never see it in this generation. It'll be many, many, many, many, hard tell, hundreds of generations yet."
But, he said, "Oh, no. The Holy Spirit told me I was going to see it."
text_no:
Hva tror du folk sa til den gamle mannen, som vi i dag ville si hadde "en fot i graven," over åtti år gammel, med hvitt skjegg, som vitnet overalt og sa: "Jeg skal ikke dø før jeg ser Herrens Kristus." Hvorfor? Han hadde rett til å tro det. Det var åpenbart for ham av Den Hellige Ånd. Det er det: åndelig åpenbart sannhet. Gud sa: "På denne klippen vil Jeg bygge Min Menighet, og dødsrikets porter skal ikke få makt over den." Dødsrikets porter skulle være imot den, men de kan ikke få makt over den, når det var åpenbart for ham at Jesus var Kristus for apostelen. Det er det.
Merk at han trodde på Gud. Kanskje den gamle mannen var en lærer med stort rykte, smart og utdannet. Hva tror du hans kollegaer, de andre forkynnerne, tenkte om ham når han gikk rundt og fortalte folk at han skulle se Kristus før han døde?
De ville si: "Det er ikke tid for Kristus. Vi kommer aldri til å se Ham i denne generasjonen. Det vil gå mange, mange, mange hundre generasjoner til."
Men han sa: "Å, nei. Den Hellige Ånd fortalte meg at jeg skulle se Ham."

text:
You know what they said? The same thing they did to Abraham, to father Abraham. When he was a hundred years old… God told him when he was seventy-five he was going to have a baby by his wife Sarah, which was sixty-five at the time. And twenty-five years they had waited for that baby, thanking God for him before the baby come. That's it. You're the children of Abraham if you have that faith. See, you've got to have the faith of Abraham.
God told Abraham, "I've called you from your land, and I'm going…" He'd lived with Sarah since she was about seventeen. And all their young lives they'd lived together. And here she was, sixty-five years old, probably thirty or four… thirty years past the menopause, as it is with ladies, far beyond any hopes of ever having a baby. But, God said, "You're going to have a baby by her. That's your wife."
text_no:
Vet du hva de sa? Det samme som de sa til Abraham, far Abraham. Da han var hundre år gammel… Gud hadde sagt til ham da han var syttifem at han skulle få en baby med sin kone Sara, som da var sekstifem. I tjuefem år hadde de ventet på den babyen og takket Gud for Ham før han kom. Slik er det. Dere er Abrahams barn hvis dere har den troen. Se, dere må ha Abrahams tro.
Gud sa til Abraham: "Jeg har kalt deg fra ditt land, og jeg skal…" Han hadde levd med Sara siden han var omkring sytten år gammel. Gjennom hele sitt unge liv hadde de vært sammen. Og her var hun, sekstifem år gammel, trolig tredve år forbi overgangsalderen, langt over noen håp om å få barn. Men Gud sa: "Du skal få en baby med henne. Det er din kone."

text:
And Abraham believed God. No matter how impossible… If you'll excuse the expression, I suppose went out and bought up a bunch of Birdseye, got the pins ready and everything, going to have the baby, making ready for it. When Sarah… The first months passed, "How are you feeling, Sarah?"
"No different."
"But praise God, we're going to have the baby anyhow." No matter what the outside says, God had made the promise. Ten years passed. "What about it?"
"Well, no different."
"But bless God, we're going to have the baby anyhow."
"How do you know?"
"God said so."
Here he is a hundred years old. And Abraham, instead of getting weak… If God gives you a promise through His Word tonight, and it don't happen in five minutes, why, you go away and say, "There's nothing to it." How can you be a son or daughter of Abraham? When God makes a promise to a son and daughter of Abraham, they believe God. And Abraham, the Bible said, instead of getting weaker, he got stronger all the time, knowing it would be more of a miracle. And he called those things which are as though they were not. He believed God.
text_no:
Abraham trodde på Gud, uansett hvor umulig det virket. Hvis du tillater uttrykket, kan jeg forestille meg at han kjøpte en mengde babyutstyr og gjorde alt klart, fordi han var overbevist om at de skulle få en baby. De første månedene gikk, og spørsmålet kom: "Hvordan føler du deg, Sarah?"
"Ingen forskjell."
"Men pris Gud, vi skal få babyen uansett." Uansett hva ytre omstendigheter sa, hadde Gud gitt sitt løfte. Ti år gikk, og spørsmålet ble stilt igjen: "Hvordan går det nå?"
"Ingen forskjell."
"Men takk Gud, vi skal få babyen uansett."
"Hvordan vet du det?"
"Gud har sagt det."
Her er han hundre år gammel. Og Abraham, i stedet for å bli svakere… Hvis Gud gir deg et løfte gjennom Hans Ord i kveld, og det ikke skjer innen fem minutter, går du kanskje bort og sier: "Det er ingenting i det." Hvordan kan du være en sønn eller datter av Abraham? Når Gud gir et løfte til en sønn eller datter av Abraham, tror de på Gud. Bibelen sier at Abraham, i stedet for å bli svakere, ble sterkere hele tiden, vel vitende om at det ville være en enda større mirakel. Han kalte de ting som ikke var som om de var. Han trodde på Gud.

text:
And Simeon perhaps said, "Now, if my father, Abraham, believed God, because God give him the promise, God promised me too, and I believe it."
What do you think a doctor would say today if an old man a hundred years old and his wife ninety, went down to the doctor's office and said, "Doctor, I want you to make arrangements at the hospital, for my wife's going to have a baby."
Why, you know what they'd do to that man? They'd say he was crazy. That's the same thing they say to anybody who takes God at His Word; they say they're crazy when they're led by the Spirit. But sons of God are led by the Spirit of God. And the Spirit of God will always bear witness of the Word being the truth. No matter what people say, the Word's right. You see it? Oh, I love the Word. Faith has to anchor on the Word, what God says.
text_no:
Og Simeon sa kanskje: "Nå, hvis min far, Abraham, trodde Gud fordi Gud ga ham løftet, så har Gud også gitt meg et løfte, og jeg tror det."
Hva tror du en lege ville sagt i dag hvis en hundre år gammel mann og hans nitti år gamle kone gikk til legekontoret og sa: "Doktor, jeg vil at du skal gjøre avtaler på sykehuset, for min kone skal ha en baby"?
Vet du hva de ville gjort med den mannen? De ville sagt at han var gal. Det er det samme de sier om enhver som stoler på Gud og Hans Ord; de sier de er gale når de ledes av Ånden. Men Guds sønner ledes av Guds Ånd. Og Guds Ånd vil alltid vitne om at Ordet er sannheten. Uansett hva folk sier, er Ordet riktig. Forstår du det? Å, jeg elsker Ordet. Troen må forankres i Ordet, i det Gud sier.

text:
Now, if you'll notice, like I was talking to you last night, Jesus didn't claim to be a healer. Jesus only saw visions. He said, "I can do nothing till the Father shows Me what to do." Saint John 5:19.
When Philip come to Him and got saved, and went over and found Nathanael way under a tree praying, several miles away, brought him back, Jesus stood and looked at him and said, "Behold, an Israelite in whom there is no guile," standing out in the audience. Knowed what was wrong with him, all about him.
He said, "How did You know me, Rabbi?"
Said, "Before Philip called you, when you were under the tree, I saw you."
That was Jesus. And those things that He did, He said, "shall you do also. I'm going away, but a little while I'll come back and be with you, even in you to the end of the world. You'll see Me, and these signs and wonders to the end of the world." Amen. Don't be excited. Amen means "so be it." All right. You won't excite me by saying, "Amen." It kind of hisses me a little bit.
text_no:
Som nevnt i går kveld, hevdet ikke Jesus å være en helbreder. Jesus så bare visjoner. Han sa: "Jeg kan ikke gjøre noe før Faderen viser Meg hva Jeg skal gjøre." (Johannes 5:19).
Da Filip kom til Ham, ble frelst og fant Natanael under et tre, flere mil unna, og førte ham tilbake, sa Jesus til Natanael: "Se, en ekte israelitt uten svik," stående ute i publikum. Han visste hva som feilte ham og alt om ham.
Natanael sa: "Hvordan kjenner Du meg, Rabbi?"
Jesus svarte: "Før Filip kalte deg, så Jeg deg under treet."
Det var Jesus. Og de tingene Han gjorde, sa Han, "skal dere også gjøre. Jeg drar bort, men om en liten stund kommer Jeg tilbake og skal være med dere, ja, i dere, til verdens ende. Dere vil se Meg, og disse tegnene og underne vil vare til verdens ende." Amen. Ikke bli opphisset. Amen betyr "så være det." Greit. Dere vil ikke forstyrre meg ved å si "Amen." Det gir meg faktisk litt ekstra energi.

text:
I know I'm talking to a many possum hunter. I had an old dog once, about take anything come along when you'd tree it but a skunk. And I'd get a skunk under the brush pile, and the dog wouldn't go get it. The only thing I'd have to do was raise up the brush pile and pat him a little bit and say, "Sic 'em." He'd go get him. The worst skunk I know is the devil. A little, "amen," once in a while, and, "sic 'em…" That's right.
All right, led by the Spirit of God. Be expecting it. Simeon was expecting God to keep His Word. Abraham was expecting God to keep His Word. David was expecting God to keep His Word when he met the giant. Samson was expecting God to keep His Word when he laid his arms around the pole. Samson was expecting God to keep His Word when he picked up a jawbone of a mule and beat down a thousand Philistines. The Hebrew children was expecting God to keep His Word when they walked into the fiery furnace. Daniel was expecting God to keep His Word when he went in among the lions. The woman that touched the hem of His garment was expecting God to keep His Word. Martha was expecting God to keep His Word when He said, "I am the resurrection and the life."
text_no:
Jeg vet at jeg snakker til mange opossumjegere. Jeg hadde en gammel hund en gang, som kunne håndtere det meste den treet, bortsett fra en skunk. Når jeg fikk en skunk under en kvistbunke, nektet hunden å hente den. Den eneste måten jeg kunne få den til å gjøre det på, var å løfte opp kvistene og klappe den litt, mens jeg sa "Angrip." Da gikk den og hentet skunken. Den verste "skunken" jeg kjenner, er djevelen. Et lite "amen" en gang iblant, og "angrip"... Det stemmer.
Vel, ledet av Guds Ånd. Forvent det. Simeon forventet at Gud skulle holde Sitt Ord. Abraham forventet at Gud skulle holde Sitt Ord. David forventet at Gud skulle holde Sitt Ord da han møtte kjempen. Samson forventet at Gud skulle holde Sitt Ord da han la armene rundt søylene. Samson forventet at Gud skulle holde Sitt Ord da han plukket opp en eselkjeve og slo ned tusen filistere. De hebraiske barna forventet at Gud skulle holde Sitt Ord da de gikk inn i den brennende ovnen. Daniel forventet at Gud skulle holde Sitt Ord da han gikk blant løvene. Kvinnen som rørte ved kanten av Hans kledning forventet at Gud skulle holde Sitt Ord. Martha forventet at Gud skulle holde Sitt Ord da Han sa, "Jeg er oppstandelsen og livet."

text:
Today, when you are trying to make Jesus just merely a man… Oh, that makes my insides feel funny: saying that Jesus was just a prophet---just a man. He was God Himself, none other than the Almighty Jehovah enveiled in flesh. If it wasn't, it was the biggest false the world ever had.
A woman, belonging to a certain church, a Christian Science church, that said to me recently, she said, "Reverend Branham, you put too much emphasis on Jesus being deity."
I said, "He was God."
She said, "Now, Reverend Branham," she said, "you know He was just a prophet."
I said, "No, He wasn't. He was God."
She said, "If I prove to you He wasn't divine, will you accept it?"
I said, "How are you going to prove it?"
She said, "By the Bible."
I said, "Sure." Yes, sir. If the Bible said He wasn't divine, He wasn't. I believe the Bible, for there is no other foundation but this right here, the Bible.
And she said, "I'll prove it by the Bible that He wasn't divine."
I said, "All right. Where's your Scripture?"
She said, "Saint John the 11th chapter."
Well, I said, "Whereabouts in there do you see that said He wasn't divine?"
Said, "When he went to the grave of Lazarus." Said, "The Bible said, 'He wept.'" Said, "That proves that He was a man. He wasn't divine, or He couldn't weep."
text_no:
I dag, når du prøver å fremstille Jesus som bare en mann… Å, det får meg til å føle meg ukomfortabel: å si at Jesus bare var en profet—bare en mann. Han var Gud Selv, selve Den Allmektige Jehova ikledd kjøtt. Hvis ikke, var det den største falskheten verden noensinne har opplevd.
En kvinne, som tilhørte en bestemt menighet, en Christian Science-menighet, sa nylig til meg: "Reverend Branham, du legger for mye vekt på at Jesus er guddommelig."
Jeg sa: "Han var Gud."
Hun sa: "Nå, Reverend Branham, du vet at Han bare var en profet."
Jeg sa: "Nei, det var Han ikke. Han var Gud."
Hun sa: "Hvis jeg beviser for deg at Han ikke var guddommelig, vil du godta det?"
Jeg sa: "Hvordan har du tenkt å bevise det?"
Hun sa: "Ved Bibelen."
Jeg sa: "Klart det." Ja, dersom Bibelen sa at Han ikke var guddommelig, var Han ikke det. Jeg tror på Bibelen, for det finnes ingen annen grunnvoll enn denne her, Bibelen.
Hun sa: "Jeg vil bevise ved Bibelen at Han ikke var guddommelig."
Jeg sa: "Greit. Hvor er skriftstedet ditt?"
Hun sa: "Johannes 11. kapittel."
Jeg sa: "Hvor i det kapittelet står det at Han ikke var guddommelig?"
Hun sa: "Da Han gikk til graven til Lasarus." Sa: "Bibelen sier: 'Jesus gråt.' Det beviser at Han var en mann. Han var ikke guddommelig, ellers kunne Han ikke gråte."

text:
I said, "Lady, your argument is thinner than the broth made out of a shadow of a chicken that starved to death." I said, "You haven't got a bit of room." I said, "He was a man, but He was God also. God was in Christ, reconciling the world to Himself." I said, "When He stood by the grave, certainly, He wept like a man. But when He looked out there, and straightened His little body up, (not much to look upon; the Bible said, 'There's no beauty we should desire Him,') but when He looked out there to the grave, a man that'd been dead and in the grave, and the skin worms crawling in and out of him, He said, 'Lazarus, come forth.' And a man that had been dead for four days, and his soul a four-days' journey out somewhere come to life and stood on his feet again. That was more than a man."
Yes, sir. Corruption knew its Master, life knew its Maker. And a man that was rotting in the grave come back as a normal living man, and sat and eat with mankind. That was more than a man. That was God in a man.
text_no:
Jeg sa: "Dame, argumentet ditt er tynnere enn buljongen laget av skyggens skygge av en kylling som sultet i hjel." Jeg sa: "Du har ikke noe grunnlag." Jeg sa: "Han var en mann, men Han var også Gud. Gud var i Kristus og forsonet verden med Seg Selv." Jeg sa: "Når Han sto ved graven, gråt Han selvfølgelig som en mann. Men når Han rettet seg opp og sa 'Lasarus, kom ut,' til en mann som hadde vært død i fire dager, med mark som allerede krøp i og ut av ham, reiste den døde seg og sto på sine egne føtter igjen. Det var mer enn en mann."
Ja, sir. Forråtnelse kjente Sin Herre, livet kjente Sin Skaper. En mann som råtnet i graven kom tilbake som en normal, levende mann og satt og spiste med menneskene. Det var mer enn en mann. Det var Gud i en mann.

text:
Surely. He come down off a mountain one night … one morning, rather, hungry, looking around over a fig tree to find something to eat. He was hungered. He was a man when He was hungered, but when He took five biscuits, and two little fishes, and fed five thousand, that was more than a man. That was God, a God-Man. Truly.
He was a man out there that night when He was so tired and the virtue going out of Him from healing the sick and so forth through the day, and God the Father speaking through Him with visions and so forth. He was out on the troubled sea. The little ship tossed about like a bottle stopper; ten thousand devils of the sea swore they'd drown Him that night. And He was so tired, He was laying in the stern of the boat; even the waves didn't wake Him up. He was a man laying there tired. But when He put His foot upon the brail of the boat, looked up, and said, "Peace, be still," and the wind and the waves obeyed Him, that was more than a man. That was the ruler of heavens and earth speaking through those mortal lips. Truly.
text_no:
Han kom ned fra fjellet en morgen, sulten, og lette etter noe å spise på et fikentre. Han var sulten som et menneske, men når Han tok fem brød og to små fisker og mettet fem tusen, var det mer enn et menneske. Det var Gud, en Gud-Menneske. 
Han var et menneske den kvelden da Han var så sliten etter at kraften hadde gått ut av Ham fra å helbrede de syke hele dagen, og Gud Faderen talte gjennom Ham med visjoner og så videre. Han var ute på det urolige havet. Den lille båten ble kastet rundt som en kork; ti tusen demoner av havet sverget at de skulle drukne Ham den natten. Han var så trøtt, Han lå i bakdelen av båten; selv bølgene vekket Ham ikke. Han var et menneske som lå der trøtt. Men når Han satte foten på ripa av båten, så opp og sa: "Fred, vær stille," og vinden og bølgene adlød Ham, da var det mer enn et menneske. Det var himmelens og jordens hersker som talte gjennom de dødelige leppene.

text:
He died on the cross screaming like a man, "My God, why hast Thou forsaken Me?" He died like a man, but on the third day He rose up, proving Himself. No wonder the poet said, "Living, He loved me; dying, He saved me; buried, He carried my sins far away; rising, He justified freely forever: Someday He's coming; oh, glorious day." No wonder it's thrilled the hearts of poets, and authors, and prophets through the years. And any man that ever amounted to a hill of beans believed on Him as Saviour.
Eddie Perronett, when he was all persecuted by the outside world… I stood recently by his grave, him and Cowper, who wrote, "There Is A Fountain Filled With Blood." When Eddie Perronett, way back in his little den room one day, and they couldn't sell his songs… They said he was crazy. But God spoke to him, and he grabbed the pen and he wrote the inauguration song of the second coming of Jesus. He said, "All hail the power of Jesus' name. Let angels prostrate fall. Bring forth the royal diadem and crown Him Lord of all." Oh, my. Look at that.
text_no:
Han døde på korset, ropende som et menneske: "Min Gud, hvorfor har Du forlatt Meg?" Han døde som et menneske, men på den tredje dagen sto Han opp, og beviste Seg selv. Ikke rart poeten sa: "Levde, Han elsket meg; døde, Han frelste meg; begravet, Han bar mine synder bort; oppstått, Han rettferdiggjorde fritt for evig: En dag kommer Han; å, herlige dag." Det er ikke rart at dette har oppildnet hjertene til poeter, forfattere og profeter gjennom årene. Og enhver mann som noen gang har betydd noe, trodde på Ham som Frelser.
Eddie Perronett, som ble forfulgt av omverdenen… Jeg sto nylig ved hans grav, han og Cowper, som skrev, "There Is A Fountain Filled With Blood." Da Eddie Perronett en dag tilbake i sitt lille krypinn, og de ikke kunne selge hans sanger… De sa at han var gal. Men Gud talte til ham, og han grep pennen og skrev inaugureringens sang om Jesu annet komme. Han sa: "All hail the power of Jesus' name. Let angels prostrate fall. Bring forth the royal diadem and crown Him Lord of all." Å, min. Se på det.

text:
Expectations: If you're expecting it, you will receive it. You usually get what you expect to get. If you come to the meeting tonight, expecting to find something to criticize the meeting, the devil will show you. He'll see that you get satisfied. You get what you expect. If you come to get a blessing, God will see that you get it. If you come to get saved, God will see you get it. If you come to get healed, be expecting to get healed; God will see that you do it. It's whatever you're expecting.
Now, in the days of Simeon, they didn't have telegrams and so forth like they do today. And they didn't have the televisions, and the radios, and so forth of modern science as we have. The message was from lip to ear. And you know, only those who're expecting usually get what they're looking for.
text_no:
Forventninger: Hvis du forventer noe, vil du motta det. Du får vanligvis det du forventer. Hvis du kommer til møtet i kveld med et ønske om å finne noe å kritisere, vil djevelen sørge for at du blir fornøyd. Du får det du forventer. Hvis du kommer for å få en velsignelse, vil Gud sørge for at du får den. Hvis du kommer for å bli frelst, vil Gud sørge for at du blir det. Hvis du kommer for å bli helbredet, forvent å bli helbredet; Gud vil sørge for at det skjer. Det handler om hva du forventer.
På Simeons tid fantes det verken telegrammer, fjernsyn eller radioer slik vi har i dag. Budskapet ble overført fra munn til øre. Og som du vet, er det vanligvis bare de som forventer noe, som får det de leter etter.

text:
Notice, there was some Magis way back over in an eastern country, India perhaps: Magis. Not long ago, about a year ago, passing through the streets of India, seeing the old Magis still in their same customs sitting there watching the stars… These Magis, they believed that He was coming. And three of them saw the star and followed it from the east to the west. The Scripture said, "We have seen His star in the east." Yes, they were in the east, and they saw it in the west, for the Orient lay east of Palestine. So, they had to be in the east to see a star in the west. "We have seen His star in the east, and we have come west to worship Him." Amen.
Notice, that star passed over every observatory. And that's the only way they could tell time then, was by the observatory. They kept time by the stars: "Watchman, what of the night?" You remember the prophet? That star… Do you believe it was a star? The Bible said it was. And it passed over every observatory with Orthodox believers looking up at the sky, watching time. They stayed up on top of the building, upon the wall, and they watched the stars. And some weary man come out at night and say, "Watchman, what about it?"
"It's so many hours," the fourth watch, the fifth watch, "so many hours till daylight."
text_no:
Legg merke til, det var noen vismenn langt tilbake fra et østlig land, kanskje India: vismenn. For ikke så lenge siden, omtrent for et år siden, gikk jeg gjennom gatene i India. Jeg så de gamle vismennene, fortsatt i sine tradisjonelle klær, sittende der og iakttagende stjernene. Disse vismennene trodde at Han skulle komme. Tre av dem så stjernen og fulgte den fra øst til vest. Skriften sier, "Vi har sett Hans stjerne i øst." Ja, de var i øst og så den i vest, for Orienten lå øst for Palestina. Så de måtte være i øst for å se en stjerne i vest. "Vi har sett Hans stjerne i øst, og vi har kommet vest for å tilbe Ham." Amen.
Legg merke til at stjernen passerte over hvert observatorium. Den eneste måten de kunne holde tiden på da var ved hjelp av observatoriet. De holdt tiden gjennom stjernene: "Vekter, hvor langt på natt?" Husker du profeten? Den stjernen... Tror du det var en stjerne? Bibelen sier det var det. Og den passerte over hvert observatorium med ortodokse troende som så opp mot himmelen og kontrollerte tiden. De oppholdt seg på toppen av bygningene, på veggen, og iakttok stjernene. Og en trøtt mann kom ut om natten og sa, "Vekter, hva skjer?"
"Det er så mange timer," den fjerde vakt, den femte vakt, "så mange timer til daggry."

text:
And they watched the stars in every city. And not one of them ever saw it. And the Magis followed it all the way from India, plumb into Palestine. Why didn't they see it? The reason people can't see the glorious things of God today, they're not looking for it. That's the reason. It's only given to those who are looking for it. Those Magis had watched Balaam's prophecy when he said, "There'll be a star rise out of Jacob." And they knew it, the way things was shaping up. It was time for it to happen. And you can tell. Amen.
Here it is. Listen. You can tell we're at the end of the road. Atomic bombs, cobalt, hydrogen, the nations is trembling, fussing, the great enemy hanging yonder with jet planes that could blow this nation to pieces, yes, the whole world in five minutes' time. Everybody's nervous.
text_no:
De så på stjernene i hver by, men ingen av dem så det. De vise menn fulgte stjernen hele veien fra India til Palestina. Hvorfor så de den ikke? Grunnen til at folk ikke ser Guds herlige gjerninger i dag, er at de ikke leter etter dem. Det er bare de som søker som får se. De vise menn fulgte Bileams profeti: "Det skal stige en stjerne opp av Jakob." De visste at tiden var inne. Og det kan du også merke. Amen.
Hør etter. Du kan se at vi er ved veis ende. Atombomber, kobolt, hydrogen, nasjonene skjelver og krangler, den store fienden venter der ute med jagerfly som kan utslette denne nasjonen, ja, hele verden på fem minutter. Alle er nervøse.

text: Like a little lamb out in the field eating, and he seems to become nervous. What's the matter? You who are up high and look down, look at there, it's a lion crouching after him. He doesn't see the lion, but there's something telling him. That's the way it is today with human beings. They're nervous. They're upset. They don't know what to do. What's the matter? The enemy's crouching. He's ready to spring. The end's here. And anybody with right kind of mental thinking knows that something's wrong.
text_no: Som et lite lam på marken som beiter, men virker nervøs. Hva er galt? Du som er høyt oppe og ser ned, se der—en løve lurer på ham. Lammet ser ikke løven, men noe advarer ham. Slik er det i dag med mennesker. De er nervøse og opprørte. De vet ikke hva de skal gjøre. Hva er galt? Fienden lurer. Han er klar til å slå til. Slutten er nær. Og alle som tenker klart forstår at noe er galt.

text:
The Magi saw that, and they said, "There'll be a star rise. I'll be watching for it." They saw it.
What did Jesus say would take place just about this time? Said, "I will restore to the people all the glory of the former, and I will pour out upon them the former and the latter rain. And the signs and wonders that was in the first church will be in the second church at the last day." Here's your sign. He's appearing on the earth. Signs and wonders, the same thing that He did is here again. His Spirit moving amongst the people.
The prophet said it'll be light in the evening. It'd be a day which was not day or night. It was gloomy. Back at the early church when they had signs and wonders of Jesus among them, they saw visions, and healed the sick, and so forth, great signs and wonders of the Bible. The light become dim. It went on till they come through the dark ages. After the Dark Age come Luther. After Luther come Wesley. After Wesley come Calvin. After Calvin come… Oh, just on and on. What is it? They had enough light to know that Jesus saved.
text_no:
Magi så stjernen og sa: "Det vil stå opp en stjerne. Jeg skal følge med." De så den.
Hva sa Jesus skulle skje omtrent på denne tiden? Han sa: "Jeg vil gjenopprette all tidligere herlighet for folket, og Jeg vil utgyte både den tidligere og den senere regn. Og tegn og under som var i den første menighet vil være i den andre menighet på den siste dag." Her er ditt tegn. Han viser seg på jorden. Tegn og under, det samme som Han gjorde, er her igjen. Hans Ånd beveger seg blant folket.
Profeten sa at det vil bli lys i kveldingen. Det skal være en dag som verken er dag eller natt. Den var dyster. Tilbake i den tidlige menighet da de hadde tegn og under av Jesus blant seg, så de visjoner, helbredet de syke, og så videre, store tegn og under fra Bibelen. Lyset ble svakt. Det fortsatte til de kom gjennom mørketiden. Etter mørketiden kom Luther. Etter Luther kom Wesley. Etter Wesley kom Calvin. Etter Calvin kom... og så videre. Hva er det? De hadde nok lys til å vite at Jesus frelser.

text:
It was a gloomy day, but the Bible said, "In the evening time, it shall be light." The sun was rising in the east on the eastern people, the Jews in the eastern people. And civilization travels with the sun. And now it's in the last days. We've traveled to the west coast. The next thing is the east again. And when the sun is going down, she'd shine out in all of her glory.
The Holy Spirit's here. Have you been expecting Him? If you are, you'll surely see His leading.
Simeon, he was expecting Jesus. And if God has made you a promise, God's under obligation to keep that promise. And God will never make a promise that He can't keep. So, when Simeon…
Perhaps that day when Jesus was born, the wise men came. They was expecting it. The poor shepherds out on the hillside… He never went to the clergymen. They wasn't expecting Him. Those shepherds, poor peasants out on the hillside, the angels came down and said, "Today, in the city of David, there's born Christ, the Lord." They went and found it even so. Messages didn't get scattered around.
text_no:
Det var en dyster dag, men Bibelen sier: "Om kvelden skal det være lys." Solen steg opp i øst, over det jødiske folk. Sivilisasjonen beveger seg med solen, og nå er vi i de siste dager. Vi har reist til vestkysten, og det neste er øst igjen. Når solen går ned, skal den skinne i all sin prakt.
Den Hellige Ånd er her. Har du ventet Ham? Hvis du har, vil du utvilsomt se Hans ledelse.
Simeon forventet Jesus. Når Gud gir et løfte, er Han forpliktet til å holde det. Gud vil aldri gi et løfte Han ikke kan holde. Så, når Simeon...
Kanskje den dagen da Jesus ble født, kom de vise menn. De forventet det. De fattige gjeterne ute på åsiden... Han gikk aldri til prestene. De forventet Ham ikke. Disse gjeterne, fattige bønder på åsiden, englene kom ned og sa: "I dag, i Davids by, er Kristus, Herren, født." De gikk og fant det slik. Budskapet spredte seg ikke.

text:
Let's take it, it's Monday morning in the temple. Many babies are born in two million people, as Jews was then. Many babies are born. Every male child must be brought to the temple after eight days and be circumcised. And the mother had to offer a lamb, or, if she was poor, she offered two turtledoves for the purification of her own self, and for the circumcision of the baby. It was a law.
Let's say it's Monday morning. Hundreds of babies is born every day. And it's Monday morning, busy around the temple. Caiaphas was sitting back with his fan as a judge, knowing nothing about it. The priests are on their regular routine, arguing what kind of buttons they should have on their clothes, or something like that. And there's an old man by the name of Simeon sitting in the prayer room, reading Isaiah, perhaps: "All we like sheep have gone astray. The Lord laid upon Him the iniquity of us all. For He was wounded for our transgressions, and He was bruised for our iniquities, and with His stripes we were healed." "Oh," he says, "God, O God, and You've give me the wonderful promise that I will see Him." About that time… If God made you a promise, God will keep His promise.
text_no:
La oss si at det er mandag morgen i tempelet. Blant to millioner mennesker, slik det var blant jødene da, blir mange babyer født. Hver mannlige baby må bringes til tempelet etter åtte dager for å bli omskåret. Moren må offre et lam, eller hvis hun var fattig, to turtelduer for sin egen renselse og for babyens omskjærelse. Dette var en lov.
Forestiller vi oss en travel mandag morgen rundt tempelet, med hundrevis av nyfødte babyer hver dag. I et hjørne sitter Caiaphas, tilbakelent med sin vifte som dommer, uvitende om det som skjer. Prestene følger sine daglige rutiner, kanskje diskuterer de hvilke knapper de skal ha på klærne. I bønnerommet sitter en gammel mann ved navn Simeon og leser kanskje fra Jesaja: "Vi fór alle vill som sauer, hver tok sin egen vei. Men Herren lot skylden som lå på oss alle, ramme Ham. Han ble såret for våre lovbrudd og knust for våre synder. Straffen lå på Ham, vi fikk fred, og ved Hans sår ble vi helbredet." 
"Oh," sier han, "Gud, å Gud, Du har gitt meg det vidunderlige løftet at jeg skal få se Ham." Omtrent på dette tidspunktet... Hvis Gud har gitt deg et løfte, vil Gud holde det løftet.

text:
The Holy Spirit spoke to him and said, "Simeon, rise up."
"What for, Lord?" No, no. A man of God don't question God. When He says, "Rise up," he raises up.
He got up. It ain't no business of what he's going to do; just follow Him. Don't wait and say, "Now, if I went to the altar … Mrs. Jones, what would she say? What would the pastor say? What would this say?" Don't make any difference what they say; do what the Holy Spirit says: "Move."
Simeon raised up in the temple. He looked around. The Bible said, "When the deep," to David, "when the deep calleth to the deep…"
Do you believe in divine healing? Say, "Amen." Well, that's the reason there is a divine healing, because you believe it. There's something in you.
text_no:
Den Hellige Ånd talte til ham og sa: "Simeon, reis deg."
"Hvorfor, Herre?" Nei, nei. En Guds mann stiller ikke spørsmål ved Gud. Når Han sier, "Reis deg," så reiser han seg.
Han reiste seg. Det er ingen sak hva han skal gjøre; bare følg Ham. Ikke vent og si, "Nå, hvis jeg gikk til alteret … Hva ville fru Jones si? Hva ville pastoren si? Hva vil de andre si?" Det spiller ingen rolle hva de sier; gjør som Den Hellige Ånd sier: "Gå."
Simeon reiste seg i tempelet. Han så seg rundt. Bibelen sier, "Når det dype," til David, "når det dype kaller på det dype…"
Tror du på guddommelig helbredelse? Si, "Amen." Vel, det er grunnen til at det finnes guddommelig helbredelse, fordi du tror på det. Det er noe i deg.

text:
When we first come, our fathers came to Kentucky, they fought the Indians. And they found the Indians, when they would die, they'd go down here at the Ohio River, and around the Green River, and Cumberland River here, and put their dead in a canoe, and put some corn, a bow and arrow, and push him out in the river. They said, "He's going to the happy hunting ground." The Indian didn't know about God; the only thing, there was something in him that told him there was a God.
The Hottentots of Africa went in there; the man didn't know right or left hand, but he worshipped an image. He knowed there was a God. Something in him… He's a human being. He's got a soul, tells him there's a God.
text_no:
Da våre forfedre først kom til Kentucky, kjempet de mot indianerne. Da indianerne døde, dro de til Ohio-elven, rundt Green River og Cumberland River, la de døde i en kano, og la ved noen mais, bue og piler, før de skjøv kanoen ut i elven. De sa: "Han skal til de evige jaktmarker." Indianerne kjente ikke Gud, men det var noe i dem som fortalte dem at det fantes en Gud.
Hottentottene i Afrika gjorde det samme. Mannen visste ikke forskjell på høyre og venstre hånd, men han tilba en avbildning. Han visste at det var en Gud. Noe i ham, som menneske, fortalte ham dette. Han hadde en sjel som fortalte ham at det var en Gud.

text:
Notice, quickly. (I have to hurry and close. Time is getting away so swiftly.) Notice, "When the deep calleth to the deep…" David said, "At the noise of Thy waterspouts…" If there is something in here calling for something, there's got to be something out there to respond to that call. In other words, before there was a fin on a fish's back, there had to be a water for him to swim in, or he wouldn't have had no fin. Before there was a tree to grow in the earth, there had to be an earth first, or there wouldn't be no tree to grow in the earth. If there's something in here calling for something, there's got to be something out there respond to it.
It was not long ago, I read in a paper (I've quoted it many times), where a little boy ate the erasers off of pencils. He ate the pedal off of a bicycle. And they took him down to the doctor and examined him at the laboratory … or, the clinic, rather, and they found out his body needed sulfur. He found sulfur in the pedal of the bicycle, or in the rubber. Sulfur is in rubber, see. There was something in here calling for sulfur, and if there's anything calling for sulfur, there's got to be a sulfur to respond to that call, or there'd never be a call.
text_no:
Vær oppmerksom, raskt. (Jeg må skynde meg å avslutte. Tiden går så fort.) Merk: "Når det dype kaller på det dype..." David sa, "Ved bruset av Dine fossefall..." Hvis det er noe her inne som kaller på noe, må det være noe der ute som svarer på den kallet. Med andre ord, før det fantes en finne på en fisk, måtte det være vann for å svømme i, ellers ville fisken ikke hatt noen finne. Før det var et tre som kunne vokse på jorden, måtte det først være en jord, ellers ville det ikke vært noe tre som kunne vokse. Hvis det er noe her inne som kaller på noe, må det være noe der ute som svarer på kallet.
For ikke lenge siden leste jeg i en avis (jeg har sitert dette mange ganger) om en liten gutt som spiste viskelærene av blyanter. Han spiste pedalen av en sykkel. De tok ham med til en lege for undersøkelse på laboratoriet... eller, klinikken, rettere sagt, og de fant ut at kroppen hans trengte svovel. Han fant svovel i sykkelens pedal, eller i gummien. Svovel er i gummi, ser du. Det var noe inne i ham som kalte på svovel, og hvis det er noe som kaller på svovel, må det være svovel som svarer på kallet, ellers ville det aldri bli noe kall.

text:
And if you're seeking divine healing from God, there's got to be a fountain open somewhere. The Indian was seeking God. There's got to be a God to respond to it. If you're seeking the Holy Spirit, there's got to be a Holy Spirit to respond to that seeking. If there's no deep to respond, there's no deep to call.
Why did you come here tonight? Because you believe that there is somewhere. And as sure as you believe it, there's a fountain open. That's why the Holy Spirit brought you here.
Simeon believed that he was going to see the Christ. And he was sitting in the temple; it was God's business to see that he found that place. Just like it's God's business to see that you got here tonight. Led, expecting, and he got up, went walking out through the building.
text_no:
Hvis du søker guddommelig helbredelse fra Gud, må det være en åpen kilde et sted. Indianeren søkte Gud. Da må det finnes en Gud som kan svare på det. Hvis du søker Den Hellige Ånd, må det finnes en Hellige Ånd som kan svare på det søket. Hvis det ikke er et dypt som kan svare, er det ingen dyp til å rope ut til.
Hvorfor kom du hit i kveld? Fordi du tror at det finnes noe et sted. Og så sikkert som du tror det, finnes det en åpen kilde. Derfor brakte Den Hellige Ånd deg hit.
Simeon trodde at han skulle se Kristus. Han satt i tempelet; det var Guds oppgave å sørge for at han fant det stedet. Akkurat som det er Guds oppgave å sørge for at du kom hit i kveld. Han ble ledet, forventet, og reiste seg, gikk ut gjennom bygningen.

text:
Notice, quickly. (Give me your attention.) Walking through the building, and the first thing you know, he hits this line of mothers. He looked standing down there. There was all the rich women with their little babies with fine needlework, their little pink and blue blankets and so forth, each one holding a lamb, pretty little things. And way down the line, I see a little virgin about eighteen years old. She's got a black name behind her, because they said this baby belonged to Joseph, and the baby was borned out of wedlock. But in that little mother's heart, she knew who that baby belonged to. It didn't have fine needlework; it had swaddling cloth. That's what you take off the back of a yolk of an ox when he's plowing: the king of glory, no needlework, swaddling cloth to wrap Him in.
The mother standing, her little veil over her face looking down to the little baby, and the other women keeping their distance. "We'll have nothing to do with her. She's an outcast." An outcast. In her heart she knew who that baby belonged to.
text_no:
Legg merke til, raskt. (Gi meg din oppmerksomhet.) Mens han gikk gjennom bygningen, stanset han ved en rekke med mødre. Han så seg rundt. Der sto de rike kvinnene med sine små babyer, innpakket i fine broderier og rosa og blå tepper. Hver og en holdt et pent, lite lam. Langt nede i rekken så han en ung jomfru, omtrent atten år gammel. Hun hadde et dårlig rykte fordi folk sa at denne babyen tilhørte Josef, og at barnet ble født utenfor ekteskap. Men i den lille morens hjerte visste hun hvem barnet virkelig tilhørte. Barnet var ikke innpakket i fine broderier, men i enkle svøp. Svøpet var det samme stoffet som brukes på ryggen av en okse når den pløyer: kongen av herlighet, uten fine broderier, bare enkle svøp til å pakke Ham inn i.
Moren sto der med et lite slør over ansiktet, bøyd over babyen, mens de andre kvinnene holdt avstand. "Vi vil ikke ha noe med henne å gjøre. Hun er en utstøtt." En utstøtt. Men i sitt hjerte visste hun hvem barnet virkelig tilhørte.

text:
The Holy Ghost said, "It shall be called the Son of God." How? It wasn't to reason; it was to believe. And there, they kept their distance. But here comes the prophet, the one who believes in supernatural. He was the supernatural. Walking down through there, the old sage, and the tears running down his cheeks as he walked, stopped right in front of that little old mother, reached over and took that baby out of her arms. He looked at it, the tears running down his cheeks, and said, "Lord, let Thy servant depart in peace. According to Your Word, my eyes has seen Your salvation." There you are. Led by the Spirit. Oh, my, expecting it, and being led by the Spirit to what you're expecting.
In the temple the same day, was an old blind prophetess by the name of Anna. She didn't leave the temple; she prayed day and night. She was blind in her eyes, but she could see a lot farther than people could with their eyes. She … blind, she was waiting for the consolation of Israel, knowing that Christ would come someday. And the Holy Spirit struck her. She stood up on her feet, and here come that old prophetess, blind, moving by the Holy Ghost, moving through the temple, around the critics, until she come and stood in front of Him. The Holy Ghost leading her to Christ. Raised up, and prophesied, and blessed Mary and the baby. Led of the Spirit, expecting it, believing it.
text_no:
Den Hellige Ånd sa: "Det skal kalles Guds Sønn." Hvordan? Det handlet ikke om fornuft, men om tro. Og der holdt de avstand. Men her kommer profeten, én som tror på det overnaturlige. Han var det overnaturlige. Da den gamle vismannen kom gående, med tårer strømmende nedover kinnene, stoppet han rett foran den lille, gamle moren, bøyde seg ned og tok babyen fra armene hennes. Han så på barnet, mens tårer rant nedover kinnene hans, og sa: "Herre, la Din tjener fare i fred. I henhold til Ditt Ord har mine øyne sett Din frelse." Der har du det, ledet av Ånden. Å, min, forventer det, og ledes av Ånden til det du forventer.
I tempelet samme dag, var det en gammel, blind profetinne ved navn Anna. Hun forlot aldri tempelet; hun ba dag og natt. Hun var blind, men hun kunne se mye lenger enn folk som hadde synet i behold. Blind som hun var, ventet hun på Israels trøst, vel vitende at Kristus en dag ville komme. Og Den Hellige Ånd traff henne. Hun reiste seg og beveget seg, ledet av Den Hellige Ånd, gjennom tempelet, forbi kritikerne, til hun stod foran Ham. Den Hellige Ånd ledet henne til Kristus. Hun reiste seg, profeterte, og velsignet Maria og babyen. Ledet av Ånden, forventende og troende.

text:
Sometime ago, coming from Dallas, Texas, a storm came up and blowed my plane. We landed in Memphis, Tennessee. I never forget it. They sent me up to that great hotel there called, "Peabody." I could never stay in a hotel like that with my own money. I don't have that type of money. But the airline sent me up there, the TWA. And then, when I was up there that night, I prayed that night. And the next morning… Do you believe that we are led by the Spirit of God?
I wish I had time to tell you many things. On with this: At this time it was very striking. The next morning I had some letters; I was going to the post office to mail them. And I started down the street; they called me and said, "Be ready to catch the plane at eight o'clock. The limousine will pick you up at seven-thirty."
I said, "Thank you, sir." I said, "Now, let's see. It's six-thirty. I got time to go down there at the post office."
text_no:
For en tid tilbake, mens jeg reiste fra Dallas, Texas, blåste en storm opp og førte til at flyet mitt måtte lande i Memphis, Tennessee. Jeg vil aldri glemme det. De sendte meg til det store hotellet der, "Peabody." Jeg kunne aldri ha råd til å bo på et slikt hotell med egne penger, men flyselskapet TWA ordnet det for meg.
Den kvelden ba jeg, og neste morgen... Tror du vi ledes av Guds Ånd? Jeg skulle ønske jeg hadde tid til å fortelle deg mange ting. Fortsetter med dette: På det tidspunktet var det veldig slående. Neste morgen hadde jeg noen brev jeg skulle poste, så jeg gikk mot postkontoret. De ringte meg og sa: "Vær klar til å ta flyet klokken åtte. Limousinen henter deg klokken halv åtte."
Jeg svarte: "Takk, sir." Jeg så på klokken og sa: "Nå, klokken er halv syv. Jeg har tid til å gå til postkontoret."

text:
I went down to mail the letters. I was going down the street singing. First thing you know, the Holy Spirit said, "Stop."
I thought, What's wrong? I thought, Maybe I just thought I heard that.
Went a little farther, and something checked me, said, "Stop a minute."
I looked, and there was a big police standing on the corner, so I stepped back up like I was looking at some fishing reels. And the Holy Spirit said, "Turn and go the other way." I don't know no more than just to do what He tells me to do, and I hope I never know any more.
I turned and started back, passed by the hotel, went on and on and on. I looked at my watch; it was eight-thirty. I got plumb over into the colored district. I didn't know where I was going; just being led. I didn't know. Just kept going. I've had it to happen dozens of times, yes, hundreds of times. I was just being led.
I thought, Lord, what do You want me way down here around the river, and these little colored haunts where the colored people live?
"That's nothing to you. Follow thou Me."
text_no:
Jeg gikk ned for å poste brevene. Jeg gikk syngende nedover gaten da Den Hellige Ånd sa: "Stopp."
Jeg tenkte: Hva er galt? Jeg tenkte kanskje at jeg bare innbilte meg det.
Jeg gikk litt videre, og noe stanset meg igjen og sa: "Stopp et øyeblikk."
Jeg så opp og la merke til en stor politimann som sto på hjørnet, så jeg skrittet tilbake som om jeg skulle se på noen fiskesneller. Den Hellige Ånd sa: "Snu og gå den andre veien." Jeg vet ikke annet enn å gjøre det Han sier til meg, og jeg håper jeg aldri vet noe mer.
Jeg snudde og begynte å gå tilbake, passerte hotellet, og fortsatte videre. Jeg så på klokken; den var halv ni. Jeg kom helt over til den fargede bydelen. Jeg visste ikke hvor jeg skulle, jeg bare ble ledet. Jeg visste ikke. Jeg bare fortsatte. Jeg har opplevd det dusinvis, ja, hundrevis av ganger. Jeg ble bare ledet.
Jeg tenkte: Herre, hva vil Du at jeg skal gjøre her nede ved elven, i disse små boligområdene hvor fargede folk bor?
"Det angår ikke deg. Følg Meg."

text:
I just kept on walking. The first thing you know, I looked, leaning across a gate… And I was going along there singing that little song, "I'm so glad that I can say I'm one of them," singing it to myself. And I looked across the gate, and there was a typical old Aunt Jemima with a man's shirt tied around her head, a little old whitewash cabin of a thing sitting there, and there was a plow point hanging on the gate for a weight to pull it back. And she was hanging out there looking over there. And I seen her about a hundred yards away, and I quit singing, just went on down the street. When I passed by, she started smiling, looking at me, tears running down her big fat cheeks. She said, "Good morning, parson."
I turned around. I said, "Good morning, Auntie." I said, "How did you know I was a parson?"
She said, "Parson, did you ever read in the Bible about the Shunammite woman, who couldn't have a baby, and she told the Lord that she was blessed, and Elijah told her that she was going to have a baby, she had it, and then the baby died?"
I said, "Yes, I remember that."
She said, "I's is that kind of woman. I couldn't have no baby, and I told the Lord I'd raise the baby," and said, "for Him." And she said, "The Lord give me and my husband a baby." And she said, "Parson," she said, "the baby, my boy, when he got to be a man, he went out and done what was wrong." And said, "I couldn't help it, parson." Said, "I've washed over the washboard" said, "and to try to raise him right in church. But," said, "he backslid, and he went away from God; and he got with the wrong crowd." And said, "Parson," said, "he got a bad disease," (Syphilitic, dying with a venereal disease.) and said, "and he's laying in here dying."
And she said, "Oh, the doctor man was here yesterday," and said, "he's been unconscious since yesterday morning, and said to me, 'He'll never gain conscious no more.' Said, 'His heart, blood is coming back through his heart. It's eat him up, and it's too late to do anything for him. It's done eat holes in him.'" And she said, "Parson, I just couldn't see my baby die without knowing the Lord." She said, "I prayed all night." Said, "I kept saying, 'Lord, I's the same woman. Where's Your Elijah?'" Said, "I kept praying, and," said, "I fell asleep." And she said, "I dreamed I seen a man coming with a lead colored suit on, wearing a tan hat." Said, "That was you, parson." She said, "The Lord told me, at three o'clock this morning, to come stand at this gate and wait for you." Said, "I've been here ever since."
I patted her on her shoulder still wet with the dew. I thought, "Lord, here it is."
text_no:
Jeg fortsatte å gå. Plutselig så jeg en kvinne som lente seg over et gjerde. Jeg gikk der og sang for meg selv den lille sangen, "Jeg er så glad at jeg kan si at jeg er en av dem." Da jeg så over gjerdet, var der en typisk eldre kvinne som hadde en manns skjorte knyttet rundt hodet. Hun satt på en liten, hvitkalket hytte. På gjerdet hang det en plogspiss som vekt for å holde det lukket. Hun stod der og kikket bortover. Jeg så henne på rundt hundre meters avstand, sluttet å synge og fortsatte bare nedover gaten. Da jeg gikk forbi, begynte hun å smile og se på meg med tårer rennende nedover de store kinnene hennes. Hun sa, "God morgen, forkynner."
Jeg snudde meg rundt og sa, "God morgen, tante." Jeg spurte, "Hvordan visste du at jeg var en forkynner?"
Hun svarte, "Forkynner, har du noen gang lest i Bibelen om kvinnen fra Sunem som ikke kunne få barn, og hun fortalte Herren at hun var velsignet, og Elia sa at hun skulle få et barn? Hun fikk barnet, men så døde barnet."
Jeg svarte, "Ja, jeg husker det."
Hun fortsatte, "Jeg er den typen kvinne. Jeg kunne ikke få barn, og jeg lovet Herren at hvis Han gav meg et barn, så ville jeg oppdra det for Ham." Hun sa videre, "Herren ga meg og mannen min en baby." Hun la til, "Forkynner, barnet mitt, min sønn, da han ble voksen, falt han inn i synd." Hun sa, "Jeg kunne ikke gjøre noe, forkynner. Jeg vasket klær på vaskebrettet for å prøve å oppdra ham riktig i menigheten. Men han falt fra og forlot Gud; han kom i dårlig selskap." Hun sa, "Forkynner, han pådro seg en alvorlig sykdom," (syfilis, døende av en kjønnssykdom) "og nå ligger han her og dør."
Hun fortsatte, "Legen var her i går," og hun sa, "han har vært bevisstløs siden i går morges, og legen sa til meg, 'Han vil aldri våkne igjen. Hjertet hans pumper blodet tilbake. Sykdommen har spist ham opp, og det er for sent å gjøre noe. Den har gjort hull i ham.'" Hun sa, "Forkynner, jeg kunne bare ikke se min baby dø uten å kjenne Herren." Hun sa, "Jeg ba hele natten." Hun sa, "Jeg fortsatte å si, 'Herre, jeg er den samme kvinnen. Hvor er Din Elia?'" Hun sa, "Jeg ba og ba, og til slutt sovnet jeg." Hun sa, "Jeg drømte at jeg så en mann komme med en grå dress og en beige hatt." Hun sa, "Det var deg, forkynner." Hun sa, "Herren fortalte meg klokken tre i morges at jeg skulle stå ved dette gjerdet og vente på deg." Hun sa, "Jeg har vært her siden."
Jeg klappet henne på skulderen som fortsatt var fuktig av dugget og tenkte, "Herre, her er det."

text:
She said, "Won't you come in?"
I've been in four king's palaces. I've been in some of the best homes in the nations around the world. I never was any more welcome than I was in that humble little home that morning. I walked in the door. There was no pin-up girls on the wall. There was a little sign up over the door, "God bless our home." I looked at an old chunk stove sitting in the floor, a little iron poster bed sitting in the corner, and a great big, about a hundred and eighty pound, dark boy laying there with a sheet in his hand going, "Mmmmm, Mmmmm."
She said, "You see, parson?" Said, "He's been unconscious, now, this is the second day." And said, "He thinks he's out on a big sea, and he's lost."
And just then he said, "Mammy, it's dark. It's dark. I just don't know where I's going."
She said, "Oh, parson," said, "do you hear that?" Said, "I can't stand to see my baby die like that," and reached down and kissed him on his forehead.
I thought, O God. No matter what he done, how much sin he was in, he's still Mammy's baby. She couldn't forget him. That's her baby. And I thought, God, if that mother can't forget her baby, yet You said, 'A mother may forget her suckling child, but I can't forget you. You're engraved on the palms of My hand. And I thought, O God, that poor woman.
text_no:
Hun sa, "Vil du ikke komme inn?"
Jeg har vært i fire kongelige palasser og noen av de beste hjemmene rundt om i verden. Jeg har aldri følt meg mer velkommen enn jeg gjorde i det beskjedne lille hjemmet den morgenen. Jeg gikk inn døren. Det var ingen pin-up-bilder på veggene. Det var en liten plakat over døren med teksten "Gud velsigne vårt hjem." Jeg så en gammel ovn stå på gulvet og en liten jernseng i hjørnet. Der lå en stor, rundt åtti kilo tung, mørk gutt med et laken i hånden som beveget seg og sa "Mmmmm, Mmmmm."
Hun sa, "Ser du, forkynner? Han har vært bevisstløs, dette er andre dagen." Hun fortsatte, "Han tror han er ute på et stort hav og har gått seg vill."
Akkurat da sa han, "Mammy, det er mørkt. Det er mørkt. Jeg vet ikke hvor jeg skal."
Hun sa, "Åh, forkynner, hører du det?" Hun fortsatte, "Jeg klarer ikke se min baby dø slik," og bøyde seg ned og kysset ham på pannen.
Jeg tenkte, O Gud. Uansett hva han har gjort, hvor mye synd han er i, så er han fortsatt Mammy's baby. Hun kunne ikke glemme ham. Det er hennes baby. Og jeg tenkte, Gud, hvis den moren ikke klarer å glemme sin baby, Du sa, 'En mor kan glemme sitt diende barn, men Jeg kan ikke glemme deg. Du er inngravert på Mine hender.' 
Og jeg tenkte, O Gud, den stakkars kvinnen.

text:
And she was crying. And I said, "Auntie, my name is Branham. Did you ever hear of me?"
She said, "No, sir, Parson Branham, I never heard of you."
And I said, "My ministry is praying for the sick."
She said, "I's glad to know that."
I said, "Auntie, will you kneel with me for prayer?"
She said, "Yes, parson."
I said, "Lead, Auntie."
And she knelt down there, and oh, brother, you talk… She might have been a wash woman, but let me tell you, when that old woman started praying, you could feel the power of God moving in the room.
She said, "Dear Lord…" Her baby laying there dying, but not a bit disturbed. She said, "Dear Lord, I comes to You in Jesus' name." Such a prayer, I stood there and took the sheet and wiped my eyes with it when she got through praying. Said, "Lord, You have sent Your parson. Now, Lord, let me hear my baby say that he's saved before he goes."
I looked at her. I thought, Bless your old heart. I said, "Now, I'll pray, Auntie." I said, "Heavenly Father, my plane's been gone nearly two hours, but You said, 'Walk.' That's all I knowed to do. I was expecting You to do something. Now, I can't do nothing, Lord, but talk to You." I just felt his feet. They was cold and sticky. Death was on him. I said, "All I can do is talk to You, Lord, but will You have mercy on this boy? Save him, dear God, and heal his body for the sake of this poor old mother."
And when I got through praying, he said, "Mama."
She said, "Yes, honey."
Says, "It's getting light in the room."
text_no:
Hun gråt. Jeg sa: "Tante, jeg heter Branham. Har du noen gang hørt om meg?"
Hun svarte: "Nei, herr pastor Branham, jeg har aldri hørt om deg."
Jeg sa: "Min tjeneste er å be for de syke."
Hun svarte: "Jeg er glad for å vite det."
Jeg sa: "Tante, vil du knele med meg for bønn?"
Hun sa: "Ja, pastor."
Jeg sa: "Led oss, tante."
Vi knelte ned, og åh, bror, du skulle ha hørt ... Hun kunne ha vært en vaskekone, men la meg fortelle deg, da den gamle kvinnen begynte å be, kunne du kjenne Guds kraft i rommet.
Hun sa: "Kjære Herre ..." Hennes barn lå der og døde, men hun var ikke det minste urolig. Hun sa: "Kjære Herre, jeg kommer til Deg i Jesu navn." For en bønn; jeg sto der og tørket øynene mine med et laken da hun var ferdig. Hun sa: "Herre, Du har sendt Din pastor. Nå, Herre, la meg høre mitt barn si at han er frelst før han går."
Jeg så på henne og tenkte: Velsign ditt gamle hjerte. Jeg sa: "Nå skal jeg be, tante." Jeg sa: "Himmelske Fader, flyet mitt har vært borte i nesten to timer, men Du sa, 'Gå.' Det var alt jeg visste å gjøre. Jeg hadde forventet at Du skulle gjøre noe. Nå kan jeg ikke gjøre noe, Herre, annet enn å snakke med Deg." Jeg følte på føttene hans. De var kalde og klissete. Døden var over ham. Jeg sa: "Alt jeg kan gjøre er å snakke med Deg, Herre, men vil Du ha miskunn med denne gutten? Frels ham, kjære Gud, og helbred hans kropp for denne stakkars gamle morens skyld."
Da jeg var ferdig med å be, sa han: "Mama."
Hun svarte: "Ja, kjære."
Han sa: "Det blir lysere i rommet."

text:
Five minutes from there, he was sitting on the side of the bed, shaking hands with me. I jumped out and grabbed a plane, and went to the station; the plane was still waiting, just making its last call. God, through grace, stopped that airplane, and kept it there because of a poor, ignorant, colored woman. Think of grace, measureless and boundless is the grace of our God. Grounded an airplane, and held it, sitting there for the prayer of a poor wash woman. Oh, my.
About a year ago, I was passing through the train … or, the train stopped. If you ever went to Memphis, how the train pulls up; I started down to get me a sandwich; it was too high on the train, so I went down to get me a hamburger. And I got out of the plane … or, train, started walking down; I heard a redcap say, "Hello there, Parson Branham."
I looked around, I said, "How do you do, sir?"
Said, "You don't know me, do you?"
I said, "I don't believe I do."
Said, "You remember that morning that the Holy Ghost led you up to my Mammy's house?"
I said, "You're not the boy?"
He said, "Yes, parson." Said, "I's healed, and I's a Christian, now." Amen. Oh, my.
text_no:
